title: Hymenophyllum album
taxonomy: Hymenophyllum album is a species of fern in the family Hymenophyllaceae. It is a type of filmy fern, which is characterized by its delicate and translucent fronds. The genus Hymenophyllum is a group of ferns that are primarily found in tropical and subtropical regions. This genus is further classified into several species, including Hymenophyllum album, which is found in various parts of the world. Hymenophyllum album is also known to be a part of the larger classification of vascular plants, kingdom Plantae.

intro: Hymenophyllum album, also known as the Silver Antler Fern, is a species of fern in the family Hymenophyllaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to its ability to boost the immune system and reduce oxidative stress.
